sections.paragraph:
[γονεύς, έως, ὁ] in our lit. only pl. οἱ γονεῖς, έων acc. τοὺς γονεῖς (as Hyperid. 3, 6; Anton. Lib. 30, 3; OGI 731, 2 [c. 200 b.c.]; SIG 796b, 13 [c. 40 a.d.]; pap [Mayser I/22, ’38, p. 30]; Pr 29:15; 4 Macc 2:10; TestSol 2:2; TestAbr B 2, p. 106, 12 [Stone p. 60]; TestLevi 13:4; TestZeb 1:3; JosAs; GrBar 4:17; ApcEsdr; Jos., Ant. 3, 92) parents (since Hom. Hymns and Hes.; ins, pap, LXX, EpArist; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 1 Jac.; Philo, Joseph., Just.) Mt 10:21; Mk 13:12 (Petosiris, Fgm. 8, 8: when the sun presents a certain appearance, there will be στάσεις πατράσι πρὸς παῖδας καὶ τὸ ἀνάπαλιν as well as many other calamities [πόλεμοι, θόρυβοι etc.]); Lk 2:27, 41, 43; 8:56; 18:29; 21:16; J 9:2f, 18, 20, 22f; Ro 1:30; 2 Cor 12:14; Eph 6:1; Col 3:20; 2 Ti 3:2; Tit 1:11 v.l.; Hv 1, 3, 1; 2, 2, 2; GJs 8:1.—B. 104. DELG s.v. γίγνομαι p. 223. M-M.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. pl. = plural

acc. acc. = accusative

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Petosiris Petosiris = astrolog. writings attributed to P. and N.: collection of fragments; before II b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hv Hv = Visions

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 205
